metadata.subject: human personality and traits
metadata.content: aggressive – agresywny: He became aggressive when provoked. ambitious – ambitny: She is ambitious and wants to become a manager. annoyed – zirytowany: I was annoyed by the loud music. arrogant – arogancki: He is so arrogant that he never admits his mistakes. artistic – uzdolniony artystycznie: She has an artistic talent for painting. bored – znudzony: He felt bored during the long lecture. brave – odważny: The brave firefighter rescued the child from the burning building. calm – opanowany: She stayed calm even when the situation got chaotic. careless – nieostrożny: His careless driving caused a minor accident. confident – pewny siebie: She felt confident before giving her presentation. depressed – przygnębiony: He has been depressed since losing his job. courageous – odważny: It takes courageous people to stand up for what is right. excited – podekscytowany: The children were excited about the upcoming trip. fearless – nieustraszony: The fearless explorer ventured into unknown territories. friendly – przyjazny: He is a friendly neighbor who always waves hello. generous – hojny: She is generous with both her time and money. honest – uczciwy: He gave an honest answer even though it was difficult. impatient – niecierpliwy: She is impatient when waiting in long lines. independent – niezależny: An independent person makes decisions without relying on others. insecure – niepewny: He feels insecure about his speaking abilities. kind – miły: She is kind to everyone she meets. lazy – leniwy: He is too lazy to clean his room. modest – skromny: Despite his fame, he remains modest. nervous – zdenerwowany: She was nervous before the job interview. proud – dumny: He is proud of his accomplishments. reliable – godny zaufania: She is a reliable teammate who always meets deadlines. respectful – szanujący: He is respectful towards his elders. shy – nieśmiały: The shy boy rarely speaks in class. thoughtful – przemyślany: She gave a thoughtful gift that suited his interests. tolerant – tolerancyjny: He is tolerant of different cultures and opinions. upset – rozgniewany: She felt upset after hearing the bad news. violent – agresywny: The movie contained many violent scenes. wise – mądry: The wise old man offered valuable advice. enthusiastic – entuzjastyczny: They were enthusiastic about the new project. caring – troskliwy: A caring teacher always supports her students. embarrassed – zawstydzony: He felt embarrassed when he tripped onstage. frustrated – sfrustrowany: She became frustrated after trying to solve the puzzle for hours. grateful – wdzięczny: He was grateful for the help he received. hopeful – pełen nadziei: She remains hopeful despite the challenges. jealous – zazdrosny: He felt jealous of his friend's success. lazy – leniwy: The lazy cat slept all afternoon. motivated – zmotywowany: She is motivated to achieve her goals. nervous – nerwowy: He felt nervous before the exam. optimistic – optymistyczny: An optimistic outlook can improve mental health. patient – cierpliwy: The doctor was patient while explaining the procedure. reliable – wiarygodny: A reliable car starts every morning without problems. sincere – szczery: She gave a sincere apology for her mistake. tidy – schludny: He keeps his desk tidy and organized. witty – dowcipny: His witty comments always make people laugh.

metadata.subject: zawody i profesje
metadata.content:
politician – a person who is involved in politics. Example: The politician gave a speech about education.
 demanding – requiring much effort or skill. Example: The job is demanding but rewarding.
 take over duties from sb – przejąć obowiązki od kogoś. Example: She will take over duties from her colleague next week.
 postal worker – a person who works for the post office. Example: The postal worker delivered the parcel this morning.
 executive – a person with senior managerial responsibility. Example: He was promoted to executive in the company.
 trade – to buy, sell, or exchange something. Example: She trades goods on the stock market.
 accountant – a professional who keeps financial records. Example: The accountant prepared the annual report.
 psychologist – a professional who studies mental processes and behavior. Example: The psychologist helped the patient cope with anxiety.
 glamorous – attractive in an exciting and special way. Example: She has a glamorous career as a fashion model.
 voluntary work – unpaid work done by choice. Example: He does voluntary work at the local shelter.
 receptionist – a person who greets visitors and answers phones. Example: The receptionist scheduled my appointment.
 high‑powered – having a lot of influence or authority. Example: She holds a high‑powered position in the firm.
 sailor – a person who works on a ship. Example: The sailor navigated through the storm.
 lucrative – producing a great deal of profit. Example: He found a lucrative job in finance.
 architect – a person who designs buildings. Example: The architect presented the new office layout.
 monotounous – boring because of lack of variety. Example: The work became monotonous after months.
 be productive – to work efficiently. Example: I try to be productive by planning my tasks.
 beautician – a professional who provides beauty treatments. Example: The beautician gave me a facial.
 purposeful – having a clear aim or intention. Example: She chose a purposeful career in medicine.
 delegate – to give responsibility to another person. Example: The manager delegated tasks to his team.
 builder – a person who constructs buildings. Example: The builder finished the house ahead of schedule.
 technical – relating to a particular subject, art, or craft. Example: The job requires technical knowledge of machinery.
 shift work – work done in different time periods. Example: He does shift work at the factory.
 bus driver – a person who drives a bus. Example: The bus driver greeted passengers with a smile.
 scientist – a person who conducts scientific research. Example: The scientist discovered a new element.
 white‑collar – relating to office work. Example: She has a white‑collar job in marketing.
 carer – a person who looks after someone who is ill or elderly. Example: The carer assisted the patient with daily activities.
 secretary – a person who handles administrative tasks. Example: The secretary organized the meeting agenda.
 hands‑on experience – practical experience. Example: Internships provide hands‑on experience.
 careers adviser – a person who helps others plan their professional path. Example: The careers adviser gave me tips for my interview.
 security – the state of being protected from danger. Example: The security guard monitors the building.
 carpenter – a person who works with wood. Example: The carpenter built a new table.
 shop assistant – a person who works in a retail store. Example: The shop assistant helped me find a size.
 chef – a professional cook, especially the chief cook in a restaurant. Example: The chef created a delicious menu.
 master a strategy – opanować strategię. Example: She mastered a strategy to improve sales.
 chemist – a scientist who studies chemicals. Example: The chemist developed a new compound.
 shopkeeper – a person who owns or runs a shop. Example: The shopkeeper greeted every customer.
 civil servant – a person employed in the public sector. Example: He works as a civil servant for the city.
 influencer – a person who influences public opinion, especially on social media. Example: The influencer promoted the new brand.
 miss deadlines – nie dotrzymywać terminów. Example: He often misses deadlines at work.
 cleaner – a person who cleans. Example: The cleaner tidied the office every evening.
 assist sb – pomagać komuś. Example: She assisted the manager during the conference.
 computer programmer – a person who writes computer code. Example: The computer programmer fixed the bug.
 supervise – to oversee a process or activity. Example: She supervises a team of designers.
 surgeon – a medical doctor who performs operations. Example: The surgeon successfully completed the operation.
 cook – a person who prepares food. Example: The cook prepared a delicious soup.
 taxi driver – a person who drives a taxi. Example: The taxi driver took us to the airport.
 counsellor – a person who gives advice. Example: The school counsellor helped the student choose a career.
 be in charge of sb/sth – być odpowiedzialnym za coś/kogoś. Example: He is in charge of the marketing department.
 dentist – a professional who treats teeth. Example: The dentist cleaned my teeth.
 ticket inspector – a person who checks tickets. Example: The ticket inspector asked to see my pass.
 work as (an engineer) – pracować jako inżynier. Example: She works as an engineer at a construction firm.
 director – a person who supervises the making of a film or who runs a company. Example: The director announced the new project.
 film – a motion picture. Example: The film won several awards.
 tour guide – a person who shows tourists around. Example: The tour guide explained the history of the city.
 be on duty – być na służbie. Example: The nurse is on duty tonight.
 be open for business – być otwartym na klientów. Example: The shop is open for business from 9 a.m.
 doctor – a qualified practitioner of medicine. Example: The doctor prescribed medication.
 translator – a person who translates from one language to another. Example: The translator helped us understand the document.
 work for (a company / sb) – pracować dla firmy/kogoś. Example: She works for an international corporation.
 economist – a specialist in economics. Example: The economist predicted a rise in inflation.
 travel agent – a person who arranges travel. Example: The travel agent booked our flights.
 be part of a team – być częścią zespołu. Example: He enjoys being part of a team.
 electrician – a person who installs electrical systems. Example: The electrician repaired the wiring.
 work freelance – pracować na własny rachunek. Example: She works freelance as a graphic designer.
 engineer – a professional who designs, builds, or maintains engines, machines, or structures. Example: The engineer tested the new bridge.
 TV presenter – a person who hosts TV programs. Example: The TV presenter interviewed the celebrity.
 be punctual – być punktualnym. Example: He is always punctual for meetings.
 farmer – a person who owns or manages a farm. Example: The farmer harvested the crops.
 be qualified – być wykwalifikowanym. Example: She is qualified to teach mathematics.
 work from home – pracować z domu. Example: Many employees now work from home.
 fashion designer – a person who designs clothing. Example: The fashion designer showcased the new collection.
 be responsible for sb/sth – być odpowiedzialnym za kogoś/coś. Example: She is responsible for the project budget.
 work in a team – pracować w zespole. Example: Collaboration helps us work in a team.
 vet – a veterinarian, a doctor for animals. Example: The vet treated the injured dog.
 firefighter – a person whose job is to extinguish fires. Example: The firefighter rescued the cat from the tree.
 vlogger – a person who creates video blogs. Example: The vlogger posted a travel vlog.
 be self‑employed – prowadzić własną działalność. Example: He is self‑employed as a consultant.
 flight attendant – a person who serves passengers on an aircraft. Example: The flight attendant offered drinks.
 work long hours – pracować wiele godzin. Example: Doctors often work long hours.
 gardener – a person who tends to a garden. Example: The gardener trimmed the hedges.
 website designer – a person who designs websites. Example: The website designer improved the site’s layout.
 work on sth – pracować nad czymś. Example: She is working on a new project.
 care for sb/sth – dbać o kogoś/coś. Example: He cares for his elderly mother.
 hairdresser – a person who cuts and styles hair. Example: The hairdresser gave me a new look.
 historian – a person who studies history. Example: The historian wrote a book about ancient Rome.
 astronaut – a person trained to travel in space. Example: The astronaut orbited the Earth.
 coordinate – koordynować. Example: She coordinates the marketing campaign.
 housewife – a woman whose main occupation is managing a home. Example: She is a housewife who volunteers at school.
 create – tworzyć. Example: Artists create beautiful works.
 work overtime – pracować nadgodziny. Example: He had to work overtime to finish the report.
 deal with sth – zajmować się czymś. Example: She deals with customer complaints.
 barrister – a lawyer who argues in court. Example: The barrister defended the client.
 instructor – a person who teaches a skill. Example: The driving instructor taught me how to change gears.
 chief executive officer (CEO) – dyrektor generalny. Example: The CEO announced the new strategy.
 work shifts – pracować na zmiany. Example: Nurses often work shifts.
 driving – prowadzenie pojazdu. Example: Driving in the city can be stressful.
 do your job (well) – wykonywać swoją pracę dobrze. Example: He always does his job well.
 work under pressure – pracować pod presją. Example: Journalists often work under pressure.
 fitness – aktywność fizyczna. Example: She goes to the gym for fitness.
 composer – a person who writes music. Example: The composer wrote a symphony.
 physical work – praca fizyczna. Example: Construction involves a lot of physical work.
 work with sb – pracować z kimś. Example: I enjoy working with my colleagues.
 do a training course – odbyć szkolenie. Example: He did a training course in project management.
 ski – uprawiać narciarstwo. Example: She likes to ski in the winter.
 conductor – dyrygentka, dyrygent. Example: The conductor led the orchestra.
 draft (a letter) – przygotować szkic listu. Example: He drafted a letter to the client.
